title: The Formation of „Å¶forms

The Formation of „Å¶forms

Before studying „Å¶forms, please review how to classify verbs as it is important in being able to successfully conjugate the verb correctly.

„Å¶form is a very important form and it's used for more grammar points in future lessons so it is important to get a good understanding of it.

A) 2nd and 3rd groups

We will start with the 2nd and 3rd groups as their conjugation is very straightforward.

You just need to substitute „Äå„Å¶„Äç for „Äå„Åæ„Åô„Äçfor both groups:

¬Ý„Åü„Å∏„Çô„Åæ„Åô ¬Ý‚áí ¬Ý„Åü„Å∏„Çô„Å¶¬Ý

„Åø„Åæ„Åô ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý‚áí „Åø„Å¶

„Åó„Åæ„Åô ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý‚áí ¬Ý„Åó„Å¶ ¬Ý

„Åç„Åæ„Åô ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý‚áí ¬Ý„Åç„Å¶

B) 1st group

There are 9 types of 1st group verbs based on the verb ending sound [k,g,s,t,m,n,b,r,w]. „Äå„Åã„ÅÑ„Åæ„Åô„Äçhas apparently no consonants, but [w] underlies and [w] disappears since [wi] does not exist in Japanese. In „Å¶forms some of these 9 types of verbs are merged, there are only 5 types of conjugation of „Å¶forms.

1) The same conjugation rule applied to 2nd and 3rd group verbs is applicable to shi¬Ýverbs„Äå„Åã„Åó„Åæ„Åô/„Åã„Åó„Å¶„Äç

2) ki/gi verbs„ÄåX„Åç„Åæ„Åô/X„Åç„Çô„Åæ„Åô„Äç are formed into „ÄåX„ÅÑ„Å¶/X„ÅÑ„Å¶„Çô„Äç respectively. ¬ÝAs you can see in the table, „Äå„Å¶/„Å¶„Çô„Äçis perfectly predictable by „Äå„Åæ„Åô„Äçforms: with two dots in „Åæ„Åôform, then also 2 dots in „Å¶form.

3) The remaining 3 groups / 6 verb cases are divided into only two types as the result of the merger.¬Ý

[(w)i ti(chi) ri] conjugate to „Äå„Å£„Å¶„Äç,

[mi, bi, ni] conjugate to „Äå„Çì„Å¶„Çô„Äç.¬Ý

In these cases, „Å£ is always followed by „Å¶, while „Çì is followed by „Å¶„Çô.

We recommend memorizing [mi bi ni] at first, because the total number of [mi bi ni] is smaller and there is only one [ni] verb,„ÄåÊ≠ª„Å´„Åæ„Åô„Äç"to die".¬Ý


4) The only exception to the above rules is „Äå„ÅÑ„Åç„Åæ„Åô„Äç. „Äå„ÅÑ„Å£„Å¶„Äç is used instead of „Äå„ÅÑ„ÅÑ„Å¶„Äç

Some Remarks for Further Study

Even if you memorize the formation rules, several weeks may be necessary for you to manage „Å¶forms. Here are some tips.

1) Confusion due to grouping error

As explained already in the section of the grouping of the verbs, it is only „ÄåXCi„Åæ„Åô„Äç(cases where more than two syllables before „Äå„Åæ„Åô„Äç and the preceding vowel is [i]) that it can not be grouped to 1st or 2nd automatically. Then we illustrate some examples of both 1st and 2nd group verbs.
